exampleSentences: người ta bán trái cây tươi ngon
người ta sửa xe máy cũ
sentenceBreakdowns: người ta (people) + bán (sell) + trái cây (fruit) + tươi ngon (fresh delicious)
người ta (people) + sửa (fix) + xe máy (motorbike) + cũ (old)

translation.en: People often walk on the street.

notes:
Người ta usually means “people” in general, like “they” or “people” in English when you don’t say exactly who. It’s vague and impersonal.

You use it before a verb to talk about what people do or think in general.  

It can also mean “other people” in contrast with you, or even “someone else” in a relationship context (a romantic rival).  

Similar idea to họ (they), but người ta is more general and less specific.

translation.en: People often walk on the street.

notes_md:
**Người ta** usually means “people” in general, like “they” or “people” in English when you don’t say exactly who. It’s vague and impersonal.

You use it before a verb to talk about what people do or think in general.  

It can also mean “other people” in contrast with you, or even “someone else” in a relationship context (a romantic rival).  

Similar idea to **họ** (they), but **người ta** is more general and less specific.

definitions.en: A phrase used to refer to people in general, without specifying anyone in particular.
definitions.vi: Cụm từ dùng để chỉ người khác nói chung, không xác định cụ thể ai.

example.en: People say that exercising is very good for your health.
example.vi: Người ta nói rằng tập thể dục rất tốt cho sức khỏe.
